title: Image To Painting
description: Turn Any Image into a Minecraft Masterpiece
body:
#
**Image-To-Painting**
![prisonpreview](https://securesector.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/2025-08-12_16.18.59.png)

Image-To-Painting lets you transform any image (from file or URL) into a fully rendered Minecraft painting using in-game maps. Whether you want to showcase pixel art, logos, or server sponsors, this plugin makes it simple, customizable, and lag-free.

See it in action before you try on my server: Play.SecureSector.org

Do not use reviews for support!

# ✔️ Get support [![ ](https://securesector.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/SS_Panel_Discord-e1755046071114.png)](https://discord.gg/pjZDdgYRJP) # **✨ Features​** Image Upload​ — Any valid online image URL​ Economy Support — Ability to add cost of maps and currency name. Multi-Map Support — Automatically splits large images into multiple map tiles for perfect detail.​ Inventory Overflow Handling — If your inventory is full, maps are safely dropped at your feet.​ High Performance — Optimized rendering with minimal server impact.​ Simple Commands — Easy to learn and use, even for new players.​ Custom Map IDs — Keep your artwork organized and reusable.​ Permission Nodes — Full control over who can give, paste, and delete paintings.​ # **⚙️ Commands​** /itp /itp list /itp help /itp place /itp delete /itp rebuild /itp give /itp give /itp render /itp frame /itp reload # **⌨️ Permissions** imagetopainting.list imagetopainting.place imagetopainting.delete imagetopainting.rebuild imagetopainting.give imagetopainting.render imagetopainting.frame imagetopainting.economy.bypass # ️️**🛠️ Config Options​** Enable/Disable price per map Ability to change price of each map and currency name Image type quality options including: dither - interpolation - multistep - sharpening Customizable width and height maxes # **🌎 Usage​** Server artwork displays Minigame arenas In-game advertisements Custom maps & adventures Museum builds # **🖼️ Image How To:** First, Place Item Frame on surface ![ ](https://securesector.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Screenshot_12.png) Next, Do /itp ​ (For this it would be /itp 1 1) ![ ](https://securesector.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Screenshot_10.png) lastly, Place the map given to you in item frame ![ ](https://securesector.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Screenshot_11.png)
